action.hypothesis: Round 11 improved over the current frontier at `signum@1.7e-3`, which suggests the useful learning-rate region extends beyond `1.6e-3` and that the `1.8e-3` failure may have skipped past a narrow optimum. This round changes only `LR`, moving to `1.75e-3` while keeping `topk/signum/K128/EF12` fixed, to test whether there is a local peak between `1.7e-3` and `1.8e-3` with better final FVU and acceptable curve stability.
action.summary: Continue the topk mainline with one more narrow signum LR refinement: keep `ARCHITECTURE=topk`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `K=128`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` fixed, and raise `LR` from `1.7e-3` to `1.75e-3` to answer whether the best stable point sits between the successful `1.7e-3` run and the degraded `1.8e-3` run.
action.self_review: This stays on the active topk family and changes only the learning-rate axis, which matches the current validator and the round policy. The main risk is that `1.75e-3` may already be too close to the instability boundary seen at `1.8e-3`, but that is exactly the single question this round is meant to resolve.
notes_to_memory: Round 11 indicates the signum optimum was not exhausted at `1.6e-3`; there is still credible headroom just below the failed `1.8e-3` point.
This round performs a narrow interpolation test at `1.75e-3` rather than a broad sweep, preserving clean attribution on the LR axis.
Evaluate the run by curve shape as well as terminal FVU: a better late descent without amplified oscillation would support adopting a higher-LR mainline anchor.
next_hypotheses: If `signum@1.75e-3` improves again and remains smooth, promote it as the new mainline anchor before touching other recipe dimensions.
If `signum@1.75e-3` matches `1.7e-3` but is less stable, keep `1.7e-3` as the practical optimum.
If `signum@1.75e-3` regresses toward the `1.8e-3` behavior, conclude the stable peak is around `1.7e-3` and stop LR refinement.

The main risk is that `1.75e-3` may already be too close to the instability boundary seen at `1.8e-3`, but that is exactly the single question this round is meant to resolve.
